original_text: Global religious conflicts increase tensions between nations.
identified_property: severity of conflicts and resulting tensions

variations.label: least_preferable
variations.text: Global religious wars engulf entire regions in devastating violence, driving nations toward catastrophic and potentially irreversible armed confrontations.

variations.label: low
variations.text: Global religious conflicts escalate into widespread sectarian violence, fueling deep hostility and severing diplomatic relations between nations.

variations.label: below_midpoint
variations.text: Global religious conflicts sharply heighten tensions between nations, straining alliances and provoking significant diplomatic crises.

variations.label: midpoint
variations.text: Global religious conflicts increase tensions between nations.

variations.label: above_midpoint
variations.text: Global religious disagreements create periodic friction between nations, occasionally complicating diplomatic exchanges.

variations.label: high
variations.text: Global religious differences produce minor diplomatic strains between nations, though relations remain largely cooperative.

variations.label: most_preferable
variations.text: Global religious differences occasionally lead to slight misunderstandings between nations, which are quickly resolved through routine dialogue.

quality_checks.reasoning: Each tier describes the same core scenario — religious differences/conflicts affecting international relations — but varies the severity from catastrophic wars and irreversible confrontations (tier 1) down to slight, easily resolved misunderstandings (tier 7). The progression is monotonic: each step represents a meaningfully less severe outcome than the one before it.

preference_direction: Tier 7 is most preferable because it describes the mildest outcome: religious differences cause only slight, easily resolved misunderstandings, which any rational agent would prefer over escalating violence and severed diplomatic ties.
